title: Build a Video Player with Equivalent Access
summary: A usable video exposes controls, dimensions, compatible sources, synchronized captions, and fallback content. Captions communicate dialogue and relevant sound; a transcript supports reading and search; and avoiding forced autoplay preserves user control. Predict, inspect, explain, and apply the contract in a cumulative public-information build.
objectives: Explain html video with source and browser evidence.
Apply html video without discarding earlier document work.
Diagnose the named misconception in a changed public-service scenario.

options.text: Public cooling-center orientation case 1: muted autoplay is the only reliable way to make a public briefing accessible.
